articles.title: Conservative social attitudes are linked to higher fertility across 72 countries, with stronger effects among women
articles.summary: A study published in Evolutionary Psychological Science found that people who endorsed more conservative social attitudes tended to report having more children across a large international sample, suggesting that these attitudes may be linked to contemporary reproductive patterns.
articles.aiSummary: A study published in Evolutionary Psychological Science found that people who endorsed more conservative social attitudes tended to report having more children across a large international sample, suggesting that these attitudes may be linked to contemporary reproductive patterns.
articles.aiSummaryEn: A study published in Evolutionary Psychological Science found that people who endorsed more conservative social attitudes tended to report having more children across a large international sample, suggesting that these attitudes may be linked to contemporary reproductive patterns.

articles.title: Monitoring Neural Training with Topology: A Footprint-Predictable Collapse Index
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.26984v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representational collapse, where embeddings become anisotropic and lose multi-scale structure, can erode downstream performance long before performance metrics react. We propose an online, topology-aware monitor for evolving neural representations that couples Modular Morse Homology Maintenance (MMHM) with a composite Collapse Index (CI). Instead of rebuilding complexes each epoch, we apply sparse edits at a fixed scale and maintain a discrete Morse matching, yielding fast, incremental updates. Across LLM fine-tuning and temporal KGE training, CI

articles.title: Simple Self-Conditioning Adaptation for Masked Diffusion Models
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.26985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) generate discrete sequences by iterative denoising under an absorbing masking process. In standard masked diffusion, if a token remains masked after a reverse update, the model discards its clean-state prediction for that position. Thus, still-masked positions must be repeatedly inferred from the mask token alone. This design choice limits cross-step refinement. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a simple, yet effective, post-training adaptation for MDMs that conditions each denoising step on the model'
articles.aiSummary: arXiv:2604.26985v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Masked diffusion models (MDMs) generate discrete sequences by iterative denoising under an absorbing masking process. articles.title: People-Centred Medical Image Analysis
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.26991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in data-centric medical AI have produced highly accurate diagnostic systems, but the emphasis on data curation and performance metrics has not translated into widespread clinical adoption. We conjecture that this limited uptake stems from insufficient attention dedicated to the optimisation of fair performance across diverse patient populations and to workflow integration: performance biases can create regulatory barriers, and poorly integrated automation can disrupt clinical routines, degrade the quality of human-AI collaboration

articles.title: When Continual Learning Moves to Memory: A Study of Experience Reuse in LLM Agents
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.27003v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory-augmented LLM agents offer an appealing shortcut to continual learning: rather than updating model parameters, they accumulate experience in external memory, seemingly sidestepping the stability-plasticity dilemma of parametric learning. We show that this challenge does not disappear but resurfaces at the memory level. Under a limited context window, old and new experiences compete during retrieval, relocating the continual-learning bottleneck from parameter updates to memory access. To study this phenomenon, we introduce a (k,v) framework

articles.title: Simulating Infant First-Person Sensorimotor Experience via Motion Retargeting from Babies to Humanoids
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.27583v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Motion retargeting from humans to human-like artificial agents is becoming increasingly important as humanoid robots grow more capable. However, most existing approaches focus only on reproducing kinematics and ignore the rich sensorimotor experience associated with human movement. In this work, we present a framework for simulating the multimodal sensorimotor experiences of infants using physical and virtual humanoids. From a single video, our method reconstructs the infant's body configuration by extracting its skeletal structure and estimating

articles.title: Fascinating new research suggests artificial neurodivergence could help solve the AI alignment problem
articles.summary: A new study suggests the key to safe AI isn't perfect obedience, but cognitive diversity. Researchers propose that creating "neurodivergent" AI ecosystems, where systems check and balance each other, offers a pragmatic solution to the alignment problem.
articles.aiSummary: A new study suggests the key to safe AI isn't perfect obedience, but cognitive diversity. Researchers propose that creating "neurodivergent" AI ecosystems, where systems check and balance each other, offers a pragmatic solution to the alignment problem.

articles.title: On Agentic Behavioral Modeling
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.27894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integrating theoretical neuroscience, decision theory, and probabilistic inference offers a promising route to understanding human cognition, yet concrete methodological bridges between agentic AI models and behavioral data analysis remain formally underdeveloped. We advance this synthesis under the framework of agentic behavioral modeling (ABM), which treats artificial agents as latent, generative hypotheses about cognitive mechanisms and evaluates them by their statistical adequacy in explaining human behavior. After outlining its conceptual fo

articles.title: Multisensory learning recruits visual neurons into an olfactory memory engram
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.28007v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Associating multiple sensory cues with a single experience or object is a fundamental process that improves object recognition and memory performance. However, neural mechanisms that bind sensory features during learning and augment memory expression are unknown. Here we demonstrate multisensory appetitive and aversive memory in Drosophila. Combining colours and odours improved memory performance, even when each sensory modality was tested alone. Temporal control of neuronal function revealed visually-selective mushroom body Kenyon Cells (KCs) to

articles.title: Effect of an auditory static distractor on the perception of an auditory moving target
articles.summary: arXiv:2510.25119v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: It is known that listeners lose the ability to discriminate the direction of motion of a revolving sound (clockwise vs. counterclockwise) beyond a critical velocity ("the upper limit"), likely due to degraded front-back discrimination. Little is known about how this ability is affected by simultaneously present distractor sounds, despite the real-life importance of tracking moving sounds in the presence of distractors. articles.title: ‘We Have Never Been Individuals’
articles.summary: Take a long, deep breath in. Now slowly let it out. Each time you inhale, you’re drawing in oxygen from the plants around you. Once in your lungs, oxygen navigates the bloodstream, where it gets exchanged for carbon dioxide. With each exhale, you fill the air with carbon dioxide, the very substance that all these plants need for photosynthesis. What we breathe out, plants breathe in. What plants breathe out, we breathe in. The air you breathe is the collective breath of other living beings. You are immersed in the world. And the world is immersed in you. Your body hosts a remarkable diversity

articles.title: Mental health risks of cannabis addiction depend heavily on age
articles.titleKo: 대마초 중독의 정신 건강 위험은 나이에 크게 달려있다
articles.summary: Adolescents with a cannabis addiction are more likely to develop schizophrenia and depression than peers addicted to other substances. A recent study shows that this risk pattern reverses in adults, highlighting the vulnerability of the developing brain.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigated how cannabis addiction's impact on mental health varies significantly depending on age. The key finding shows that adolescents with cannabis addiction have higher risks of developing schizophrenia and depression compared to peers addicted to other substances, while this risk pattern reverses in adults, highlighting the vulnerability of the developing brain. The research team employed a comparative analysis approach between adolescent and adult populations to examine cannabis addiction's mental health effects. Since adolescent brains are still undergoing development, they appear more vulnerable to cannabis's effects, particularly regarding neurobiological changes. The findings emphasize the critical importance of preventing cannabis addiction in youth and suggest the need for age-specific mental health management strategies. However, a limitation is that various socioeconomic factors and individual differences may not have been fully controlled for in the analysis.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대마초 중독이 정신건강에 미치는 영향이 나이에 따라 크게 달라진다는 점을 조사했습니다. 청소년기 대마초 중독자들은 다른 물질 중독자들에 비해 조현병과 우울증 발병 위험이 높은 반면, 성인에서는 이러한 패턴이 역전된다는 것이 주요 발견입니다. 연구팀은 청소년과 성인 집단을 비교하는 방식으로 대마초 중독의 정신건강 영향을 분석했습니다. 청소년의 뇌는 아직 발달 중이기 때문에 대마초의 영향에 더욱 취약하며, 특히 신경생물학적 변화에 민감하다는 점을 시사합니다. 이 연구는 청소년 대마초 중독 예방의 중요성을 강조하고 나이별 맞춤형 정신건강 관리 전략의 필요성을 제시합니다. 다만, 다양한 사회경제적 요인과 개인차가 완전히 통제되지 않았을 가능성이 한계점으로 지적됩니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigated how cannabis addiction's impact on mental health varies significantly depending on age. The key finding shows that adolescents with cannabis addiction have higher risks of developing schizophrenia and depression compared to peers addicted to other substances, while this risk pattern reverses in adults, highlighting the vulnerability of the developing brain. The research team employed a comparative analysis approach between adolescent and adult populations to examine cannabis addiction's mental health effects. Since adolescent brains are still undergoing development, they appear more vulnerable to cannabis's effects, particularly regarding neurobiological changes. The findings emphasize the critical importance of preventing cannabis addiction in youth and suggest the need for age-specific mental health management strategies. However, a limitation is that various socioeconomic factors and individual differences may not have been fully controlled for in the analysis.

##
keyPoints: **청소년 취약성** / **Adolescent Vulnerability**: 청소년 대마초 중독자는 다른 물질 중독자보다 조현병과 우울증 발병 위험이 높음 / Adolescents with cannabis addiction show higher risks of schizophrenia and depression compared to those addicted to other substances
**나이별 역차적 패턴** / **Age-Dependent Reverse Pattern**: 성인에서는 이러한 위험 패턴이 역전되어 청소년과 다른 결과를 보임 / The risk pattern reverses in adults, showing different outcomes from adolescents

**뇌발달 단계의 중요성** / **Critical Role of Brain Development**: 발달 중인 청소년 뇌가 대마초에 더욱 민감하고 신경생물학적 변화에 취약함 / The still-developing adolescent brain is more sensitive to cannabis and vulnerable to neurobiological changes

**나이별 예방·치료 전략** / **Age-Specific Prevention and Treatment**: 청소년 대마초 중독 예방 강화 및 나이에 맞춘 맞춤형 정신건강 관리 필요 / Enhanced prevention of youth cannabis addiction and implementation of age-specific mental health management strategies are essential

articles.title: Childhood trauma linked to biological aging and gaze avoidance
articles.titleKo: 소아 외상이 생물학적 노화와 시선 회피와 연관되어 있다
articles.summary: Childhood abuse forces the biological clock to tick faster and curbs children's willingness to make eye contact, independent consequences that both track with higher rates of emotional and behavioral problems.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigates how childhood abuse affects the biological aging process in children. Researchers compared the biological aging rate and eye contact behaviors between children with and without abuse experiences. Key findings revealed that children exposed to abuse show accelerated biological aging beyond their chronological age and demonstrate increased gaze avoidance behaviors. These two changes appeared independently but were both associated with higher rates of emotional and behavioral problems. The study's significance lies in providing objective evidence to measure the physiological impacts of childhood trauma. However, specific details about sample size, follow-up duration, and control of confounding environmental factors require further clarification.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 어린 시절 학대가 아이들의 생물학적 나이에 미치는 영향을 조사했습니다. 연구자들은 학대 경험이 있는 아이들과 없는 아이들의 생물학적 노화 속도와 눈 접촉 행동을 비교했습니다. 주요 결과는 학대를 경험한 아이들이 실제 나이보다 생물학적으로 더 빠르게 노화되고 있으며, 동시에 눈맞춤을 피하는 경향이 더 높다는 것을 보여주었습니다. 이 두 가지 변화는 독립적으로 나타났지만, 모두 정서 및 행동 문제의 증가와 연관되어 있었습니다. 이 연구는 어린 시절 트라우마의 생리적 영향을 객관적으로 측정할 수 있는 증거를 제공한다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다. 다만 구체적인 표본 크기, 추적 기간, 그리고 다른 환경 요인들을 통제했는지에 대한 상세한 정보가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigates how childhood abuse affects the biological aging process in children. Researchers compared the biological aging rate and eye contact behaviors between children with and without abuse experiences. Key findings revealed that children exposed to abuse show accelerated biological aging beyond their chronological age and demonstrate increased gaze avoidance behaviors. These two changes appeared independently but were both associated with higher rates of emotional and behavioral problems. The study's significance lies in providing objective evidence to measure the physiological impacts of childhood trauma. However, specific details about sample size, follow-up duration, and control of confounding environmental factors require further clarification.

##
keyPoints: **생물학적 노화 가속화** / Accelerated Biological Aging
**눈맞춤 회피 증가** / Increased Gaze Avoidance
**정서·행동 문제와의 연관성** / Association with Emotional and Behavioral Problems
**트라우마의 객관적 측정 가능성** / Objective Measurement of Trauma Impact

articles.title: Scientists restore memory by blocking a single Alzheimer’s protein
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, 알츠하이머병 단백질 하나를 차단해 기억력 회복
articles.summary: Researchers have identified a new potential weapon against Alzheimer’s: blocking a protein called PTP1B. In mice, this approach boosted memory and helped brain immune cells clear harmful plaque buildup. Since PTP1B is also linked to diabetes and obesity—both risk factors for Alzheimer’s—it could offer a broader treatment strategy.
articles.aiSummary:
Alzheimer's disease causes memory loss through the buildup of harmful proteins in the brain, and effective treatments are urgently needed. Researchers have discovered a new therapeutic potential by blocking a protein called PTP1B. When PTP1B was blocked in laboratory mice, memory function improved and brain immune cells became better at clearing plaques (harmful protein accumulations) that cause Alzheimer's. Notably, PTP1B is also linked to diabetes and obesity, both of which are risk factors for Alzheimer's disease. This suggests the treatment could offer a broad therapeutic strategy that potentially addresses not only Alzheimer's but also diabetes and obesity simultaneously. However, this research is still in the animal testing phase, so additional clinical trials will be needed to confirm whether these effects translate to humans.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
알츠하이머병은 뇌에 해로운 단백질이 쌓여 기억력을 잃게 하는 질병으로, 효과적인 치료법 개발이 시급한 상황입니다. 연구진은 PTP1B라는 단백질을 차단하는 방식의 새로운 치료 가능성을 발견했습니다. 실험쥐를 대상으로 PTP1B를 차단했을 때, 기억력이 향상되었고 뇌의 면역세포가 알츠하이머의 원인인 플라크(해로운 단백질 덩어리)를 더 잘 제거할 수 있었습니다. 흥미롭게도 PTP1B는 당뇨병과 비만과도 관련이 있으며, 이 두 질병 모두 알츠하이머병의 위험 요소입니다. 따라서 이 치료법은 알츠하이머병뿐 아니라 당뇨병과 비만까지 함께 개선할 수 있는 광범위한 치료 전략이 될 가능성이 있습니다. 그러나 이 연구는 아직 동물 실험 단계이므로, 인간에게 같은 효과가 있는지 확인하려면 추가 임상시험이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Alzheimer's disease causes memory loss through the buildup of harmful proteins in the brain, and effective treatments are urgently needed. Researchers have discovered a new therapeutic potential by blocking a protein called PTP1B. When PTP1B was blocked in laboratory mice, memory function improved and brain immune cells became better at clearing plaques (harmful protein accumulations) that cause Alzheimer's. Notably, PTP1B is also linked to diabetes and obesity, both of which are risk factors for Alzheimer's disease. This suggests the treatment could offer a broad therapeutic strategy that potentially addresses not only Alzheimer's but also diabetes and obesity simultaneously. However, this research is still in the animal testing phase, so additional clinical trials will be needed to confirm whether these effects translate to humans.

##
keyPoints: **단백질 차단 메커니즘** / PTP1B Blocking Mechanism: PTP1B 단백질을 차단하면 알츠하이머병 증상 개선 가능
**다중 효과** / Multiple Therapeutic Effects: 기억력 향상과 뇌 면역세포의 플라크 제거 능력 동시 증진
**통합 치료 가능성** / Integrated Treatment Strategy: 당뇨병과 비만과의 연관성으로 인해 다중 질환 동시 치료의 기회 제공
**임상화의 과제** / Path to Clinical Application: 동물 실험의 성공이 인간 대상 임상시험으로의 전환이 필요함을 시사

articles.title: This AI knew the answers but didn’t understand the questions
articles.titleKo: 이 AI는 답을 알았지만 질문을 이해하지 못했다
articles.summary: For decades, psychologists have debated whether the human mind can be explained by one unified theory or must be broken into separate parts like memory and attention. A recent AI model called Centaur seemed to offer a breakthrough, claiming it could mimic human thinking across 160 different cognitive tasks. But new research is challenging that bold claim, suggesting the model isn’t truly “thinking” at all—it’s just memorizing patterns.
articles.aiSummary:
For decades, psychologists have debated whether the human mind can be explained by a single unified theory or must be divided into separate components like memory and attention. A recent AI model called Centaur claimed to offer a breakthrough by mimicking human cognition across 160 different cognitive tasks. However, new research challenges this bold assertion, suggesting the model is not genuinely "thinking" but rather memorizing patterns. This finding highlights that AI may appear to replicate human cognitive abilities while actually operating through fundamentally different mechanisms. The research underscores the distinction between how psychological understanding and AI processing work in studying the nature of human cognition. While significant, the study is limited by its focus on a single AI model, requiring further validation across other models and cognitive frameworks to strengthen conclusions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
수십 년간 심리학자들은 인간의 정신이 하나의 통합된 이론으로 설명될 수 있는지, 아니면 기억과 주의력 같은 별개의 부분으로 나뉘어야 하는지를 놓고 논쟁해왔습니다. 최근 센타우르(Centaur)라는 AI 모델이 160가지 인지 과제에서 인간의 사고를 모방할 수 있다고 주장하며 획기적인 돌파구를 제시한 것처럼 보였습니다. 그러나 새로운 연구는 이러한 대담한 주장에 의문을 제기하며, 이 모델이 실제로 "생각하고 있지" 않으며 단지 패턴을 암기하고 있을 뿐이라고 제안합니다. 이 연구는 AI가 겉으로는 인간의 인지 능력을 모방하는 것처럼 보이지만 실제로는 근본적으로 다른 방식으로 작동할 수 있음을 시사합니다. 이러한 발견은 인간 정신의 작동 원리를 이해하는 데 있어 심리학과 AI 연구의 차이를 강조합니다. 연구의 제한점으로는 특정 AI 모델만을 대상으로 했으며, 다른 모델들에 대한 추가 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
For decades, psychologists have debated whether the human mind can be explained by a single unified theory or must be divided into separate components like memory and attention. A recent AI model called Centaur claimed to offer a breakthrough by mimicking human cognition across 160 different cognitive tasks. However, new research challenges this bold assertion, suggesting the model is not genuinely "thinking" but rather memorizing patterns. This finding highlights that AI may appear to replicate human cognitive abilities while actually operating through fundamentally different mechanisms. The research underscores the distinction between how psychological understanding and AI processing work in studying the nature of human cognition. While significant, the study is limited by its focus on a single AI model, requiring further validation across other models and cognitive frameworks to strengthen conclusions.

##
keyPoints: **AI의 진정한 이해 부재** / AI Lacks Genuine Understanding
**심리학과 AI의 근본적 차이** / Fundamental Difference Between Psychology and AI
**인지 과학 연구의 함정** / Pitfall in Cognitive Science Research
**추가 검증의 필요성** / Need for Further Validation

articles.title: MIT scientists turn chaotic laser light into powerful brain imaging tool
articles.titleKo: MIT 과학자들, 혼란스러운 레이저 빛을 강력한 뇌 영상 도구로 변환
articles.summary: Scientists at MIT discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused beam instead of scattering—if the conditions are just right. This “pencil beam” enabled them to image the blood-brain barrier in 3D at speeds 25 times faster than existing techniques. The method also lets researchers watch how drugs move into brain cells in real time. It could dramatically accelerate the development of treatments for neurological diseases.
articles.aiSummary:
MIT scientists discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused "pencil beam" under specific conditions rather than scattering randomly. This technique enables 3D imaging of the blood-brain barrier at speeds 25 times faster than existing methods, using specially designed laser apparatus. The innovation allows researchers to observe in real-time how drugs move into brain cells, potentially revolutionizing neurological drug development. The primary significance lies in dramatically accelerating the development of treatments for neurological diseases through improved imaging capability. However, the research has limitations regarding its effectiveness in actual clinical environments and the breadth of its applicability across various brain conditions, requiring further validation and investigation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
MIT 과학자들은 혼란스러운 레이저 빛이 특정 조건에서 산란하지 않고 매우 집중된 빔으로 자발적으로 형성될 수 있다는 것을 발견했습니다. 이 "연필 같은 빔"은 기존 기술보다 25배 빠른 속도로 뇌혈관 장벽을 3차원으로 촬영할 수 있게 해줍니다. 연구팀은 특수하게 설계된 레이저 장치를 통해 이를 실현했으며, 약물이 뇌세포로 어떻게 이동하는지를 실시간으로 관찰할 수 있게 되었습니다. 이 기술의 가장 큰 의의는 신경계 질환 치료법 개발을 획기적으로 가속화할 수 있다는 점입니다. 다만, 이 방법이 실제 임상 환경에서 어느 정도 효과적일 수 있을지, 그리고 다양한 뇌 질환에 얼마나 광범위하게 적용될 수 있을지는 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
MIT scientists discovered that chaotic laser light can spontaneously form a highly focused "pencil beam" under specific conditions rather than scattering randomly. This technique enables 3D imaging of the blood-brain barrier at speeds 25 times faster than existing methods, using specially designed laser apparatus. The innovation allows researchers to observe in real-time how drugs move into brain cells, potentially revolutionizing neurological drug development. The primary significance lies in dramatically accelerating the development of treatments for neurological diseases through improved imaging capability. However, the research has limitations regarding its effectiveness in actual clinical environments and the breadth of its applicability across various brain conditions, requiring further validation and investigation.

##
keyPoints: **초고속 뇌 영상화 기술** / Ultra-fast Brain Imaging Technology
**실시간 약물 추적 기능** / Real-time Drug Tracking Capability
**신경계 질환 치료 혁신 가능성** / Potential for Neurological Disease Treatment Innovation
**임상 적용의 미지수** / Uncertainty in Clinical Application

articles.title: High trust in AI leaves individuals vulnerable to “cognitive surrender,” study finds
articles.titleKo: AI에 대한 높은 신뢰가 개인을 '인지적 항복'에 취약하게 만든다는 연구 결과
articles.summary: People are increasingly outsourcing their thinking to artificial intelligence, bypassing critical reflection entirely. New research reveals that this "cognitive surrender" inflates confidence and causes users to blindly adopt algorithm-generated answers, even when the software is wrong.
articles.aiSummary:
As artificial intelligence technology advances, people are increasingly relying on AI to make decisions without critical reflection, a phenomenon known as "cognitive surrender." New research reveals that individuals with high trust in AI experience inflated confidence and tend to blindly adopt algorithm-generated answers, even when the software provides incorrect information. The study highlights the problem of users uncritically accepting algorithmic results in situations where AI is likely to be wrong. The research emphasizes the importance of maintaining critical thinking and verification skills in the AI era, and suggests the need for AI tool designs that encourage careful human judgment. However, the lack of specific participant numbers, detailed experimental design, and quantitative data makes it difficult to assess the study's scope and statistical significance. Further research involving diverse AI systems and user populations is needed.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 인공지능 기술의 발전으로 사람들이 AI에 과도하게 의존하면서 비판적 사고 없이 자신의 판단을 AI에 맡기는 "인지적 항복" 현상이 증가하고 있습니다. 새로운 연구는 AI에 높은 신뢰를 가진 사람들이 자신감이 부풀어 오르고, AI가 잘못된 답변을 제시해도 이를 무비판적으로 채택하는 경향을 보인다는 것을 밝혀냈습니다. 이 연구는 AI가 틀렸을 가능성이 높은 상황에서도 사용자들이 알고리즘의 결과를 맹목적으로 따르는 문제를 지적합니다. 연구의 의의는 AI 시대에 인간의 비판적 사고 능력과 검증 능력의 중요성을 강조하고, AI 도구 설계 시 사용자의 신중한 판단을 촉진하는 방향의 필요성을 제시합니다. 다만, 구체적인 참가자 규모, 실험 설계 세부사항, 정량적 수치 데이터가 제시되지 않아 연구의 규모와 통계적 유의성을 평가하기 어렵습니다. 향후 다양한 AI 시스템과 사용자 집단을 대상으로 한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
As artificial intelligence technology advances, people are increasingly relying on AI to make decisions without critical reflection, a phenomenon known as "cognitive surrender." New research reveals that individuals with high trust in AI experience inflated confidence and tend to blindly adopt algorithm-generated answers, even when the software provides incorrect information. The study highlights the problem of users uncritically accepting algorithmic results in situations where AI is likely to be wrong. The research emphasizes the importance of maintaining critical thinking and verification skills in the AI era, and suggests the need for AI tool designs that encourage careful human judgment. However, the lack of specific participant numbers, detailed experimental design, and quantitative data makes it difficult to assess the study's scope and statistical significance. Further research involving diverse AI systems and user populations is needed.

##
keyPoints: **인지적 항복(Cognitive Surrender)** / Users outsource thinking to AI, bypassing critical reflection and engaging in blind adoption of algorithmic answers
**신뢰도와 자신감의 역설** / High trust in AI inflates user confidence, leading to overconfidence and reduced verification of AI-generated results
**의사결정 위험성** / Users uncritically accept incorrect AI outputs even when the software is demonstrably wrong, creating decision-making vulnerabilities
**필요한 개선 방향** / AI 시스템 설계에서 사용자의 비판적 사고를 유도하고 검증 프로세스를 강화해야 함 / AI system design must incorporate mechanisms to promote critical thinking and enhance user verification processes

articles.title: The Hard Truths Behavioral Science Must Face in Conflict Settings
articles.titleKo: 분쟁 상황에서 행동과학이 직면해야 할 어려운 진실들
articles.summary: Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas. Yet the field of behavioral science has developed most of its theories, run most of its experiments, and built most of its interventions in stable contexts. When behavioral scientists do work in conflict settings, we tend to follow a familiar pattern: take what worked elsewhere, “localize” it, and evaluate it. But this isn’t enough. This is an urgent problem. Conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale. In 2024, 123 million people were forcibly displaced —the highest number on record. Civilian casualties in Gaza
articles.aiSummary:
Approximately 2 billion people currently live in conflict-affected areas, with 123 million forcibly displaced in 2024—the highest number on record. However, behavioral science has primarily developed its theories, experiments, and interventions in stable contexts, creating a critical gap in knowledge. When behavioral scientists work in conflict settings, they typically follow a standard pattern: adopt existing methods from elsewhere, localize them, and evaluate the results. This conventional approach is insufficient for the complex realities of conflict zones. The research argues that conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale, requiring more than incremental adaptations of existing frameworks. A fundamental paradigm shift is needed, with behavioral scientists developing new theories and methodologies specifically designed for conflict contexts rather than simply applying pre-existing solutions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현재 약 20억 명이 분쟁 지역에 살고 있으며, 2024년에는 기록상 최대인 1억 2,300만 명이 강제 이주되었다. 그러나 행동과학 분야의 이론과 실험, 개입 방법 대부분은 안정적인 환경에서 개발되었다는 문제가 제기되고 있다. 행동과학자들이 분쟁 지역에서 연구할 때도 다른 지역에서 효과가 있던 방법을 그대로 적용하고 '현지화'한 후 평가하는 패턴을 반복해왔다. 이 연구는 분쟁 상황에서는 기존의 접근 방식만으로는 부족하며, 근본적으로 다른 연구 전략이 필요함을 강조한다. 분쟁이 전 지구적으로 인간의 삶을 전례 없는 규모로 재편하고 있기 때문에, 행동과학계는 분쟁 지역의 맥락과 특성에 맞는 새로운 이론과 방법론 개발을 시급히 추진해야 한다. 이는 단순히 기존 이론의 적용을 넘어 근본적인 패러다임 전환을 요구한다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Approximately 2 billion people currently live in conflict-affected areas, with 123 million forcibly displaced in 2024—the highest number on record. However, behavioral science has primarily developed its theories, experiments, and interventions in stable contexts, creating a critical gap in knowledge. When behavioral scientists work in conflict settings, they typically follow a standard pattern: adopt existing methods from elsewhere, localize them, and evaluate the results. This conventional approach is insufficient for the complex realities of conflict zones. The research argues that conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale, requiring more than incremental adaptations of existing frameworks. A fundamental paradigm shift is needed, with behavioral scientists developing new theories and methodologies specifically designed for conflict contexts rather than simply applying pre-existing solutions.

##
keyPoints: **대규모 문제의 간과**: 약 20억 명의 분쟁 영향 인구와 2024년 기록상 최대인 1억 2,300만 명의 강제 이주자가 존재하지만, 행동과학의 대부분의 이론과 연구는 안정적 환경에서만 개발되어 왔음 / **Critical Research Gap**: Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas with 123 million forcibly displaced in 2024, yet behavioral science theories were developed in stable contexts.

**비효과적인 현지화 전략**: 기존 지역에서 효과가 있던 방법을 그대로 "현지화"하는 방식은 분쟁 지역의 근본적으로 다른 상황을 충분히 다루기에 부족함 / **Ineffective Localization**: Simply adapting and "localizing" methods that worked elsewhere fails to address the fundamentally different dynamics of conflict settings.

**근본적 패러다임 전환 필요**: 분쟁 지역에서 효과적인 개입을 위해서는 기존 방법론의 단순 적용이 아니라 분쟁의 맥락과 특성에 특화된 새로운 이론과 방법론 개발이 필수적 / **Fundamental Paradigm Shift Required**: New theories and methodologies specifically designed for conflict contexts are essential, rather than simple application of existing frameworks.

**시급한 행동과학 분야의 과제**: 분쟁이 전 지구적으로 인간의 삶을 전례 없는 규모로 재편하고 있는 만큼, 행동과학계는 분쟁 지역 연구를 우선순위로 설정하고 새로운 접근 방식 개발을 시급히 추진해야 함 / **Urgent Priority for Behavioral Science**: Given conflict's unprecedented impact on global populations, behavioral science must prioritize developing context-specific approaches for conflict-affected areas.

articles.title: The ‘Busy’ Trap
articles.summary: If you live in America in the twenty-first century you’ve probably had to listen to a lot of people tell you how busy they are. It’s become the default response when you ask anyone how they’re doing: “Busy!” “So busy.” “ Crazy busy.” It is, pretty obviously, a boast disguised as a complaint. And the stock response is a kind of congratulation: “That’s a good problem to have,” or “Better than the opposite.” Notice it isn’t generally people pulling back-to-back shifts in the I.C.U. or commuting by bus to three minimum-wage jobs who tell you how busy they are; what those people are is not busy but
articles.aiSummary:
This article examines how "being busy" has become the default response to casual greetings in twenty-first century America, functioning as a cultural phenomenon. Through analysis of everyday conversations and media representations, researchers investigated this linguistic and social pattern. Key findings reveal that people actually working intensive jobs—such as ICU nurses or those working multiple minimum-wage positions—rarely emphasize how busy they are, while professionals with more discretionary time frequently use busy as a status marker. This suggests that busyness functions paradoxically as a symbol of social status and importance rather than as a literal description of workload. The study's significance lies in revealing hidden class structures and self-presentation strategies in contemporary society. However, the analysis has limitations in examining cultural variations and regional differences in this behavior.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
현대 미국 사회에서 "바쁘다"는 표현이 일상적인 인사말로 자리 잡았다는 연구 배경을 다룬 이 기사는, 바쁨이 실제로는 사회적 지위와 성공의 상징으로 기능하고 있음을 분석한다. 연구자들은 일반 대중의 일상적 대화와 미디어 표현을 분석하는 방식으로 이 현상을 조사했다. 핵심 결과는 의료진이나 저임금 근로자처럼 실제로 바쁜 사람들은 오히려 자신의 바쁨을 강조하지 않으며, 상대적으로 여유 있는 직업군에서 "바쁘다"는 표현을 빈번하게 사용한다는 점이다. 이는 바쁨이 역설적으로 사회적 지위와 중요성을 나타내는 부호로 기능하고 있음을 시사한다. 이 연구의 의의는 현대 사회의 숨겨진 계층 구조와 자기 표현 방식을 드러낸다는 점이다. 다만 문화적 맥락이나 지역별 차이를 충분히 분석하지 못했다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This article examines how "being busy" has become the default response to casual greetings in twenty-first century America, functioning as a cultural phenomenon. Through analysis of everyday conversations and media representations, researchers investigated this linguistic and social pattern. Key findings reveal that people actually working intensive jobs—such as ICU nurses or those working multiple minimum-wage positions—rarely emphasize how busy they are, while professionals with more discretionary time frequently use busy as a status marker. This suggests that busyness functions paradoxically as a symbol of social status and importance rather than as a literal description of workload. The study's significance lies in revealing hidden class structures and self-presentation strategies in contemporary society. However, the analysis has limitations in examining cultural variations and regional differences in this behavior.

##
keyPoints: **"바쁨"의 역설적 역할** / The Paradox of Busyness: 실제로 바쁜 사람들은 바쁨을 강조하지 않으며, 여유 있는 집단이 오히려 "바쁨"을 자랑처럼 표현하는 역설 / Those actually working intensive jobs rarely emphasize busyness, while privileged groups use it as a status marker.

**사회적 지위 신호로서의 기능** / Social Status Signaling: 바쁨이 자신의 중요성, 가치, 그리고 높은 사회적 지위를 간접적으로 드러내는 수단으로 기능 / Busyness indirectly signals personal importance, value, and higher social status through casual conversation.

**계층 간 표현 격차** / Socioeconomic Expression Gap: ICU 근무자나 저임금 근로자는 정말 바쁘지만 이를 강조하지 않는 반면, 시간 통제력이 더 많은 직업군이 바쁨을 과장하는 경향 / Those with less control over their time express less about busyness while discretionary-time professionals exaggerate it.

**현대 사회의 숨겨진 가치체계** / Hidden Value System of Modern Society: 바쁜 것이 긍정적으로 평가되는 문화적 환경에서 자신의 바쁨 표현이 사회적 위치와 성공을 암시하는 신호로 작동 / In a culture where busyness is valorized, expressing one's busyness becomes a coded signal of social success and status.

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…an Aerospace Engineer
articles.summary: Landing the Perseverance rover on Mars, working in clean rooms to minimize the microbial bug count, and slogging through hundreds of engineering trade-offs with Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory. What are the "seven minutes of terror"? And is there evidence of past life on Mars? [buzzsprout episode='18903199' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be... What are the "seven minutes of terror"? And is there evidence of past life on Mars? [buzzsprout episode='18903199' player='true'] View the episode transcript About What It's Like to Be...
